news.title: Investor expects solid Q2 results despite inflation concerns
news.summary:
STORY: Wall Street climbed on Monday as investors turned their focus to the upcoming corporate earnings season.

"We expect Q2 results to be quite good, but we are very focused on hearing what companies are saying about the pressures from those higher oil prices this spring, how it's flowing through, how they're adapting," said Miller. "Are we going to see higher prices? Are we going to see lower margins from these companies?"

Miller says earnings calls will provide a clearer picture of whether companies have been able to absorb higher costs or pass them on to consumers.

"We won't be surprised to see inflation stay where it is or even move a little higher over the next couple of months," she added.
